briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag devoid of deadstick pauses.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag d
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Fishes Carolina rig in 4 to 25 feet of water, but most of the time works it in 8 to 12 feet; holds rod to his side at three o'clock position during entire retrieve.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

KVD: 'Biggest smallmouth cranking difference: it's NOT about bottom contact. Keep crankbait a few feet off bottom — smallmouth in clear water love to feed looking up. It's about speed and covering water, casting all directions to find roaming aggr...
Robertson: In fall, smallmouth stack up on boulder fields in 20 feet of water where baitfish are concentrated. The fish can be found within a very small 60-foot area when they're grouped up.
Hackney: 'Big fish relate to the perimeter, a creek channel, and hold on an isolated piece of grass on a flat. Little fish run up shallow while big fish satellite around the area.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
